entry.title: Re: Workzone Header toolbar functions not working
entry.content: @divyanshu_jaiswal1: The version was update by SAP, we didn't do anything.

entry.title: Re: link sales order reservation to refurbishment order
entry.content:
hello @Vivek_Savant

you understood perfectly, well done 🙂

about the item category: we created specific item category in order to have the following:

header of BOM (programmed board) is NOT MRP relevant but it is delivery and billing relevant

item of BOM (non programmed board) is MRP relevant but not delivery and not billing relevant.

Also, the header component has procurement type SE to be able to use it in the refurbishment order as costing relevant.

is this sufficient or you need additional info?

entry.title: New GL migration - differences detected by /NGL/COMPARE_LEDGERS
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

we have differences detected by /NGL/COMPARE_LEDGERS while running our NewGL migration cockpit.

For one specific GL account it could be one single document, posted prior to DEM/EUR currency conversion back in 1999. Some lines have been migrated, others not (?)

Other GL accounts might face even more documents/errors.

Any experience out of your migration sessions regarding further analysis or how to proceed would be highly appreciated.

Thanks and regards,
Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: Ariba Network Invoicing with OCR
entry.content: Thanks a lot Pushpendu!

entry.title: Re: With the new revamped SCN from where I can access the...
entry.content: @AnnePetteroe Thanks for this. Just one suggestion, having a separate section as "My Bookmarks" under "My settings" would have been Great! Presently its hidden under "Subscriptions and Notifications" thats the reason it got missed.

entry.title: Re: Create a Launcher fails
entry.content: Found out that this means that there are input types that are linked to "Start", so they are expected to be given from the outside of the automation and that´s why a launcher is not applicable.

entry.title: Sustainability and Ethical Sourcing in Procurement
entry.content:
Imagine a future where every product we use helps make the world a better place. This vision is becoming real as more companies adopt sustainable and ethical procurement practices. By making smart choices about what they buy, companies can have a big impact on the environment and society.

Why is Sustainability in Procurement Important?

Sustainability in procurement means making smart choices that help the Earth. This includes thinking about how much energy a product uses, if it can be recycled, and its overall impact on the environment. Sustainable procurement not only protects the environment but also improves a company's reputation, efficiency, and innovation. Companies that focus on sustainability often save money, attract top talent, and build stronger relationships with stakeholders.

The Importance of Environmental Impact

Choosing eco-friendly products and materials is crucial. For example, a furniture company aims to use only recycled and renewable materials by 2030, which will greatly reduce its environmental footprint. Sustainable procurement helps companies follow environmental laws and avoid legal problems, as seen with the European Union's guidelines for green purchasing.

Cost Savings and Consumer Behavior

Sustainable products can lead to significant cost savings. Energy-efficient products lower energy bills, and reducing waste cuts disposal costs. Additionally, more consumers are choosing environmentally friendly products. A survey showed that 73% of consumers are willing to change their habits to reduce their environmental impact. Companies that respond to these preferences can gain a competitive advantage and build brand loyalty.

Ethical Sourcing and Social Responsibility

Ethical sourcing ensures that products are made under fair conditions, including fair pay and safe working environments. This practice supports good labor practices and helps communities. For instance, an outdoor clothing company has been a leader in ethical sourcing, ensuring their supply chain follows the highest labor standards.

Implementing Sustainable and Ethical Procurement

To implement sustainable and ethical procurement, companies should set clear sustainability goals, create policies, engage stakeholders, evaluate environmental impact, and conduct regular audits. Training procurement teams and working with suppliers are also important steps. Technological innovations like AI and IoT can further enhance transparency and traceability in supply chains.

Connecting to SAP Ariba and SAP Procurement Solutions

SAP provides integrated solutions that help manage sustainability and ethical sourcing throughout procurement processes. SAP Ariba, for instance, enables businesses to connect with a global network of suppliers who follow sustainable and ethical standards. It helps assess suppliers and ensures they meet a company’s sustainability criteria. Additionally, SAP Ariba's advanced analytics and reporting tools allow organizations to monitor and improve their procurement practices continuously.

The SAP Business Network connects buyers and suppliers on a global scale, promoting transparency and collaboration. It supports sustainable and ethical procurement by providing a platform where companies can share best practices, innovation, and compliance information. This network enhances supply chain resilience and operational efficiency, contributing to the overall sustainability goals of the organization.

Future Trends in Sustainable Procurement

Looking ahead, several trends will shape the future of sustainable procurement. These include stricter environmental regulations, changes in consumer expectations, a focus on the circular economy, and advancements in technology. Companies that embrace these trends will thrive in today's market.

Conclusion

Sustainability and ethical sourcing are essential for responsible and forward-thinking organizations. By adopting these practices, companies can help create a sustainable and fair world while enjoying significant business benefits. As more people care about the environment and social issues, companies that embrace sustainability will thrive.

Best regards

Lars

entry.title: Enable Now Producer Functionalities
entry.content:
Hi together,

we are facing some questions of our customer and didn't find answers in any documentation.

  • Are there standard templates available in the producer or does the customer needs to create custom templates for E-Learnings?
  • Is there any example scorm content generated with the producer to show to the customer and test the integration with their LMS?
  • Are there functions like interacting persons who can be inserted in the book pages?

Thank you very much!

Romina

entry.title: How to change the billing status for an outbound delivery?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have an outbound delivery with goods movement done. But the billing status still shows A, how to complete the follow-up process? Actually I want this delivery "done".

Regards,

Eric

entry.title: Best Practices for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Best Practices: SAP BTP ABAP Environment

Introduction: 

This blog post summarizes the best practices for getting started with the SAP BTP ABAP environment (aka Steampunk). Further articles and blog posts are linked in the appropriate places so that more information is available there if required.  

The SAP BTP ABAP environment was developed to create cloud-enabled business applications, services and extensions. It offers the ABAP cloud development model that uses SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Fiori and a cloud-optimized ABAP language with standard APIs. Developers can use a toolset for tight integration with Git-enabled lifecycle management. 

The use of SAP BTP ABAP environment offers 3 key benefits:

  • It enables a side-by-side extension option for the custom extension of SAP S/4HANA
  • The cloud transformation can be accelerated by using reusable ABAP assets to create innovative solutions for the cloud
  • Keep the core clean, the on-premise custom code is kept on another decoupled platform, where you can profit from independent and regular release cycles

The following graphic shows the four different areas of use of the SAP BTP ABAP environment. Individual adaptations for each interest group are possible for each part so that the best possible benefit is achieved. 

SAP BTP ABAP Environment Positioning

Development

You can simply connect the ABAP Cloud system to Eclipse so that you can use the ABAP Development Tools (ADT) provided there to develop the ABAP Cloud apps. Build your own OData services using the ABAP RESTful application programming model optimized for SAP HANA Cloud. You can also analyze the ABAP code with advanced custom code migration tools and evaluate the effects of conversion to SAP S/4HANA, migration to SAP BTP or during the clean core journey. 

Administration and Integration

Administrative tasks such as user management and security setup can be performed via the role-based SAP Fiori Launchpad. Create extension projects between ABAP Cloud and other SAP Build tools, and easily integrate with existing on-premises systems and any SAP S/4HANA systems. You can also use third-party services that are based on HTTP. 

The central aspect of the SAP BTP ABAP environment is the broad range of data centers globally provided. You can choose from a total number of more than 20 data centers, a list of all of them can be found in the SAP Discovery Center. The number and therefore the variety will continue to increase, and further locations are planned and will soon be available. All other upcoming features can be found in the roadmap and the what's new page for the SAP BTP ABAP environment in the SAP Help Portal. In addition, there is already a blog post in which the upgrade window for next year's releases is listed and you can find information regarding the Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS).

The Landscape Portal is part of the SAP BTP ABAP environment offering and acts as a central tool to perform system administration (for example system hibernation), manage lifecycle operations, and facilitate the configuration and deployment of multitenant SaaS solutions.  

Choosing Your Starting Point: The Free Tier Option 

It is important to note at the initial stage that it is not necessary to create all the systems at once, but only those that are needed at the time. On the one hand, this ensures clarity and, on the other, saves unnecessary costs.  

We recommend using the Free Tier option for your first contact with this system. This allows you to try out everything free of charge and then decide whether you want to continue using it. This offer can be used for a maximum of 90 days. 

Setting Up Your System: Step-by-Step Guide 

The next step is the creation of a software component as a delivery unit for your project, this is possible via the SAP Fiori Launchpad. The free tier system can be converted into a standard plan development system, so that all projects and created files can be transferred. It is of course also possible to create a development system directly. The SAP BTP Cockpit is generally used for the system creation. 

With the ABAP Development Tools (ADT) for Eclipse, all items can be developed as usual and released using the transport requests. 

Once development is complete, a test system can be created via the SAP BTP Cockpit. The software components can then be pulled into the test system in the SAP Fiori Launchpad. 

Best Practices for System Management  

A subaccount to create a system is required. We recommend creating a separate subaccount for each system. This ensures maximum flexibility, for example with the trust settings. All information about Cloud Foundry and the required accounts and spaces can be found in the SAP Help Portal.

The next step is to define a region in which the system is located. There are three different hyperscalers to choose from, which are represented in more than 20 data centers worldwide. 

And each system must be assigned to a Cloud Foundry Space. Here too, it is recommended to use a Cloud Foundry Space for only one system to create a better overview. 

Optimizing Costs: Sizing and Hibernation Tips  

All systems should initially be created with the minimum size, one ACU (ABAP Compute Unit) and 2 HCUs (HANA Compute Unit). This usually provides sufficient coverage, as one ACU can serve around 1,000 active business users per day. These are the only two metrics that are relevant for this. The size can of course be easily changed and adapted at a later stage vie the SAP BTP Cockpit. An overview of the prices can be found in the SAP Discovery Center, where the ACUs and HCUs are also explained in more detail. 

If the system has more than just the minimum size available, it is possible to use the elastic scaling. The resources are adjusted and automatically scaled based on the required workload. To be able to use this functionality, the BTPEA Commercial Model is required. 

The default setting when creating a system is a development system, but if you want to create a test or productive system, you must uncheck this box. To distinguish between the different systems, you can assign them a descriptive ID in the “ABAP System ID” field. 

The system hibernation function can be used to save further costs. This function is suitable for stopping development systems outside working hours, for example at weekends, starting test systems only when they are needed or starting the productive system only when it goes live. It can simply be started up via the Landscape Portal. 

A detailed cost analysis for customers can be found in this blog post. 

Recommended System Landscapes: Finding the Right Fit 

The various landscapes are only briefly explained here, detailed descriptions can be found in the documentation. When setting up the systems, we recommend that you only start with the system that is currently required. It is not necessary to set up the entire system landscape directly. It is sufficient if only the development system is set up first and then the test or production system as soon as the need is met. On the one hand, this provides a better overview at the beginning and on the other hand, costs can be reduced. 

3-System Landscape 

This landscape consists of a development system, a quality assurance system and the production system. This allows the tests of new developments to be tested directly in parallel and ensures that the application is also executable in non-development systems. 

5 System Landscape 

In this scenario, the code lines for development and corrections can be handled separately. The correction system and the test system are on the same level as the production system. This means that the respective corrections can be tested before they are imported into the production system. To develop larger releases and code parts, there is an actual development system. There is also the corresponding test system. Urgent corrections can be implemented directly and have no influence on the normal development cycle. 

Note: At this point you can use the system hibernation function, because if it is not necessary to enter corrections frequently, the correction system and the associated test system can be shut down and thus also save costs. If there is a need for these systems, they can simply be started up again. A detailed explanation of the system hibernation can be found in this blog post. 

This learning journey  can give you an overview of how to create an ABAP environment on the SAP BTP or you can attend our monthly Customer and Partner Roundtable. Among other topics, new product features are presented in this meeting, live demos are shown by experts or you have the opportunity to ask questions live. To register for the roundtable, please follow the SAP Community for SAP BTP ABAP Environment, where the next date will be announced in a blog post and registration details will be provided. If you have missed a roundtable or would like to look at past ones again, you can find them on GitHub, where the respective slides are also available. 

Hopefully this blog post has given you a clear introduction to the SAP BTP ABAP environment.

entry.title: Re: link sales order reservation to refurbishment order

entry.title: Re: Hybris commerce quote Flow

Please can some one help me how can I achieve and asagent can see approve or  reject option for quote which is submitted by b2b user?

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP RAP : Custom Entities with compositions relationship in a Fiori Elements App

Thanks in Advance.

Regards,
Arasu

entry.title: Santa's Early Gift for SAP BTP
entry.content:
At this time of year, as many of us are looking forward to some fun over the festive period, I like many people am busy wrapping presents for friends and family. But in the fine spirit of giving, the rather nice team at G2, playing the role of Santa in their smart red and white livery, have given the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) team an early Christmas gift, in the form of Winter 2025 badges, celebrating SAP BTP’s recognition as a Leader across a number of different categories in the G2 Winter 2025 reports. This includes a Momentum badge this time around too, indicating that our customers place us in the top 25% of leaders in certain product categories. What a nice surprise for the team, and a great way to round out 2024!

The recognition in new G2 reports builds on that received just a few short months ago in their Fall 2024 reports, and shows a continued appreciation from SAP’s customers for the value they derive from using SAP BTP, as badges are offered only following a robust scoring methodology which ranks vendor solutions based on real, verified, and trusted software buyers’ reviews. This of course gives the SAP BTP team a great feeling of pride, and gratitude too, knowing that our customers see us in this way. And, I know I express this for the whole team, when I say “thank you” to the SAP customers who left reviews with G2 that enabled us to achieve this recognition.

Where are customers feeling the value from SAP BTP?

Of course, SAP customers use SAP BTP for a variety of business needs, and so the value they feel from the platform and services, flows to them in a number of different ways. Some of this value is described in the reviews that customers leave at the SAP BTP landing page on the G2 website. Here are a few examples:

“It effortlessly connects different systems and makes everything work together smoothly. It also gives you the tools to get creative, build what you need and adapt as your business grows…flexibility for implementation is superb and the support from SAP makes it better because of which it’s very easy and convenient.”

(User review by a mid-market customer, Dec 2024)

 

“The feature I appreciate the most in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) is its multifunctionality and integration. It is possible to optimize business processes through a single platform which integrates several SAP and third-party systems together. Thanks to the cloud-based business services offered on the Platform, along with powerful analytics and ML capabilities, it is an excellent environment for building and releasing out-of-the-box solutions. And finally, the low-code/no-code environment allows business users themselves to create the required solutions and thus speed up digital change.”

(User review by small business customer, Sept 2024)

 

“SAP business technology is very User friendly & is an end to end solution…It basically solved all my procurement transformation needs.”

(User review by enterprise customer, Dec 2024)

So, as 2024 draws to a close, I’d like to thank both G2 for recognizing SAP BTP as a Leader in many of the 2024 reports as well as the new Winter 2025 reports, and of course thanks to our customers too, for all of the continued kind words and support, and for choosing SAP BTP as the platform to help run your business.

Thanks for a super end to 2024, and here’s to a wonderful 2025 ahead!

Chris Grundy, Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: Wrapping up 2024 for Application Development, Automation and Integration on SAP BTP: What a year!
entry.content:
2024 has been an incredibly busy year for our application development, automation and integration portfolio on the SAP Business Technology Platform. As the year draws to a close, I wanted to take some time to reflect on our achievements. Indeed, I am very grateful to the people in my teams who have worked very hard to deliver so many improvements to our solutions, and I would like to say a big thank you to all of them.

Of course, you've heard a lot about Joule, our AI co-pilot, over the course of the year. In fact, we have been integrating AI in general, and Generative AI with Joule in particular, into all components of our portfolio, as we announced earlier. And we demonstrated our AI capabilities at SAP TechEd two months ago. This deployment of Business AI has generated great interest from our customers and partners, as well as analysts.

But we've also introduced many new features and enhancements, which I'm proud to present in detail below.

SAP Build

In terms of application development and automation, in addition to embedding AI in all of our solutions, our biggest improvement in 2024 was to enrich SAP Build with pro-code capabilities. Indeed, we announced SAP Build Code at SAP TechEd 2023, made available in March this year, and we recently added ABAP Environments to SAP Build. SAP Build now allows business users and developers to work across their low-code, Java/JavaScript and ABAP projects from a unique starting point, the SAP Build lobby, benefitting of seamless interoperability capabilities fostering fusion development.

With this, SAP Build is now THE extensibility solution for any SAP customer: you can now accelerate your ERP extension development and modernization, foster innovation, and automate processes, all in a single, comprehensive suite of AI-driven tools for extension development. And SAP S/4HANA users can create extensions with SAP Build directly from the ERP business application, thanks to our new Extensibility Wizard for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This year, we have worked hard to improve the integration of the SAP Build components, both with other components of SAP BTP and with other SAP solutions: for example, SAP Build Process Automation is now integrated with SAP Build Apps and also with Advanced Event Mesh, applications created with SAP Build Apps can be deployed in SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Work Zone supports multi-tenancy.

Finally, I would like to mention that we have improved the usability of SAP Build, for example with a new process layout and script task editor in SAP Build Process Automation and guided experiences in SAP Build Work Zone.

This image summarizes all the improvements we delivered this year:

A few weeks ago, Gartner has recognized SAP as a Visionary in its 2024 Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms (LCAP). I would like to thank the SAP Build team for achieving this great result!

You can find more details in these blog posts from our product experts:

  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – November 2024
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – September 2024
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – July 2024
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Q2 2024 Edition
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Seamless Integration of Processes and Apps
  • What’s New with SAP Build Process Automation: January 2024

SAP Integration Suite

In 2024 we have also improved SAP Integration Suite in many different areas:

Regarding SAP PI/PO migration, which is a topic on which I published a blog post earlier this year to explain Advanced Event Mesh and Edge Integration Cell, we introduced a Pipeline concept and new rules added for migration assessment.

Edge Integration Cell is now available on Google Cloud and Red Hat OpenShift. It supports outgoing connections through a HTTP proxy and provides offline availability during periods of temporary connectivity loss.

This year, Event Mesh became a capability of SAP Integration Suite, which also allows direct consumption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Events and distributed tracing, from sending to receiving applications across one or multiple event brokers.

We have been investing heavily in improving developer efficiency and operational excellence. A few of Gen AI Increments that we recently launched were to accelerate development of integration flows with Gen AI and analyze APIs using API Anomaly detection in API Management

You can also accelerate connectivity with SAP Integration Suite using new adapters for Coupa, NetSuiteERP, SnowFlake, and Amazon DynamoDB, SMB, Jira, Amazon EventBridge, HubSpot, Anaplan with many more planned to be released soon.

We enhanced Cloud Integration and API Management user experience with SAP Horizon theme. Enriched the Cloud Integration capability with the ability to inspect content size for all the integration artifacts, manage access at the package level, validate integration flow design as per the guidelines in integration flow editor.

Furthermore, API Management now supports multiple target endpoints in API proxy tooling and recently launched Developer Hub for search, discovery and consumption of APIs. We also launched oData provisioning as a new capability.

We have onboarded 25+ partners with a contract and further enriched our marketplace with 53+ new partner content in particular 7 new APIs, 4 Processes, 20+ Integration packages and 22+ Validated partner use cases added on SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Finally, B2B Scenarios for Trading Partner Management (TPM) and Integration Advisor have also been enriched with functionalities like archiving of Payload data for Trading Partner Management scenarios, EDI flat file payloads can be directly used to simulate mappings, for easier access to envelope fields these can be added in the Integration Advisor, simplified mass-maintenance in TPM, support for custom SOA messages in Integration Advisor, new Tradacoms message standard.

You can find more details in these blog posts from our product experts:

  • Whats New For SAP Integration Suite – August 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – June & July 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – Spring 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – February 2024
  • What's New for SAP Integration Suite - January 2024

Feel free to follow our #WhatsNewInSAPIntegrationSuite on SAP Community and register for our webinar for updates on upcoming innovations!

Finally, I’m also proud to share that with SAP Integration Suite SAP has been named a Leader for 4th consecutive time by Gartner® in its 2024 Magic Quadrant™ for iPaaS. In addition, SAP is a Leader in IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Business-to-Business Middleware 2024 Vendor Assessment.

Kudos to the team for these achievements!

What’s for 2025?

As you’ve seen above, we delivered a lot of new features and enhancements in 2024. And for sure we will continue in 2025!

You can find out what we have planned for next year on our public roadmap for application development, automation and integration on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

With that, I wish you a Merry Christmas and very happy holidays!  🎅🏻🎄

entry.title: [Group Reporting] creating subitem categories
entry.content:
According to help.sap the subitem category in Group Reporting is fixed to 1 (transaction types) or 2 (functional area), see Define Subitems | SAP Help Portal

However in the system it is possible to create more subitem categories and when setting default values or in breakdown categories I can select other subitem categories than 1 and 2.

In my specific case we have subitems for certain balance sheet fs items that are not aggregated on carry forward.

Is there a reason only subitem categories 1 and 2 should be used? Or can I create more categories without issues in the system afterwards?

entry.title: Re: Replication Flow Blog Series Part 7 – Performance
entry.content:
hello, there is possible an error in the page,

the table DHCDC_RMSOBJSTG do not exists, instead the table DHAPE_RMSOBJSTG exists.

the error is the same in the referred note https://me.sap.com/notes/3388742.

Do you confirm or I need to be worried about it in the system I tested it now (S/4HANA 2021 with CNV_NOTE_ANALYZER_DI implemented)?

entry.title: MDG DQM Validation Rules – Access additional data model tables without ABAP coding
entry.content:
Background

If you want to implement medium or complex validations it’s often necessary to involve data and attribute values from tables that are not available by direct access in the rule. In the past (BAdI/coding approach) this was also called "cross entity" and it caused enhanced coding and data model knowledge. In MDG DQM this is now very easy because the framework gives you the ability to access the data by using predefined procedure calls.

This blog explains how you can access additional data model tables using these procedure calls so that this implementation can be used in all MDG processes – even to access current change request data.

Introduction

When the system calls the implementation of a validation rule, the data of the base table, the parent table of the base table, and the root table is automatically supplied.

An example for the business partner data model: If the base table is Phone (ADR2), the data from the Address (ADRC) and the General Data (BUT000) tables is automatically supplied.

An example for the product data model: If the base table is Storage Location (MARD), the data from the Plant Data (MARC) and the Basic Data (MARA) tables is automatically supplied.

But how to access data that resides in a different table of the data model?

The answer in a nutshell: Use the delivered procedure calls in BRFplus in the applications ZMDQ_147 (for business partners) and ZMDQ_194 (for products).

Steps for creating a validation rule using delivered procedure call

Let’s take an example: Create a validation rule that checks that for a business partner of category Organization either the tax number category DE0 or DE1 is assigned. This rule shall be executed in a change request.

Step 1: Create validation rule with base table General Data (BUT000)

Use the Validation Rules for Business Partners app to create a rule with some business details and base table General Data (BUT000).

Note that the base table is not Tax Numbers (DFKKBPTAXNUM) for the following reason: During execution the system loops over all entries of the rule base table – but if there is no entry (in this example: if there is no tax number at all), no error is raised.

Step 2: Add rule usage and prepare BRFplus artefacts

Add rule usage Check in Change Requests and choose Prepare.

Step 3: Implement rule scope Partner Category is equal to Organization

Navigate to the BRFplus Workbench using the link for the rule scope expression in the Implementation section. Select the field Partner Category from the context and set the value to ‘2’ (Organization).

Step 4: Implement rule condition Assigned Tax Number Category is equal to DE0 or DE1

In the following, we explain how to access data from the process table DFKKBPTAXNUM_PRC creating a table operation that uses the delivered procedure call DFKKBPTAXNUM lookup.

  • Navigate to the BRFplus Workbench using the link for the rule condition expression in the Implementation section.
  • Create a table operation:

 

 Choose Create and Navigate to Object and confirm the upcoming popup windows. 

 

Choose the operation Has at least.

To access data from table DFKKBPTAXNUM select the procedure call DFKKBPTAXNUM lookup.

 

With the next step, you need to define the selection conditions for the tax type category.

Do not forget to save your expression.

Note: Process ID, Step Number, Source System and Source ID are automatically filled during execution.

Step 5: Approve rule and enable usage

Go back to the Validation Rules app, approve the rule and enable the desired usages.

Step 6: Test rule in a change request

Using this procedure call ensures that the current data is checked for the different usages. This includes the change request as well and saves you from having to call up the change request API.

Using the Check action in a change request shows the predefined message if the data violates the rule:

Summary

These procedure calls are available for the other data model tables as well. If you cannot find them in your BRFplus application you might need to update the BRFplus infrastructure using report MDQ_UPD_INFRASTRUCTURE_VAL. If the report is not yet available in your system refer to note 3279689.

Using these procedure calls will simplify the implementation of your validation rules. As mentioned before – they are available for product master data as well.

Cheers

Sabine

entry.title: Re: /SCWM/RWEIGHT_VOLUME_UPDATE in Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear Colleague,

We have somehow located the root cause of the issue. Unfortunately the sequence of your setup is wrong and the system does not block this. The storage bin migration is done before the "Define Warehouse Attributes" (either transported or manually maintained). This causes the storage bin does not have the unit information and thus causes the issue. I think your productive environment has not been setup and please make sure the correct sequence is followed.

We are testing internally if we can use the existing report to correct this. I will update here.

Kind Regards,

Clark

entry.title: Re: Embedded EWM Opportunistic Cross-Docking
entry.content:
Regarding 1.9.1.

After SAP S/4HANA 2023 Release you cant use /SAPAPO/MAT1 for this Settings. You have to use MM02.

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/how-to-edit-product-group-product-group-type-after-sap-s-4-hana-2023/ba-p/13794915

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 Reuse Change Document Component OData V4
entry.content: I'm also interesed in the solution for this, especially with the Change Document. Can you post the solution here?

entry.title: Re: Future Dated Hires Provisioning from SF EC/Onboarding to Azure AD / Onprem AD
entry.content:
@vslavova1 Yes - the JSON path for startDate must be set to 
$.employmentNav.results[-1:].startDate

Then future hires will sync with all data, also DisplayName - and then CN should be populated and users created

entry.title: Re: Issue with User Creation for SAP Mobile Start
entry.content:
Hi @Skylark,

this is Dennis, I'm a consultant working on topics around SAP Mobile Start. 
Please see me answer below: 

From your question it's clear that for now you are using the SAP Default Identity provider (SAP Universal ID) for all the users. This method is usually only used for Admins, as the Universal ID accounts are used to log into the SAP BTP, do the Setup and Admin work on a Global or Subaccount level. 

End-users usually don't access any services via the SAP Default Identity Provider. You would instead use the SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication (short IAS). The IAS is SAP's IdP offering for customers. You should have an own IAS tenant in which you create and manage users. This IAS tenant connects to your Subaccount and end-users will login with their IAS accounts. 

It's also possible to make use of a corporate IdP that you might already use in your company. (e.g. Microsoft Entra, Octa...). In this case you would use the IAS as a proxy to your corporate IdP and have end-users logging in via their known account that might already be in use. 

If this is a new topic for you / your company I would recommend to reach out to your SAP Contact or SAP Partner to request help on this. User Management or Security topics should be looked at in more detail to find a fitting solution for your landscape and plan ahead correctly. 

Hope that is helpful! 
Best regards, 
Dennis

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Language of Folders
entry.content:
"Its not a bug, its a feature!"

I received Feedback from Signavio Support:
"Hello Stephen,

Please note that this is a known limitation of the system. The same is mentioned in the following KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/3161333/E

As this is a limitation, I have just reported your feedback to our product management as a feature request.

I currently have no information about the implementation of this feature request. However, you can get an insight into the development plans on the page https://roadmaps.sap.com/ by using the term "Signavio" for the search.

If there is an improvement of the feature, that will be part of the official communication and via the release notes of the products.

Thanks and best regards,
Kristof
SAP Signavio Customer Support"

Therefore I will leave this ticket open. But currently (18th Dec 24) there is no solution for this but hopefully it will be in the future!

entry.title: How to call RFC FM using Service Consumption Model in ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Please follow the below step by step instructions.

  1. **Download RFC Metadata**:

   - Open transaction ACO_PROXY on your ERP ex: S4HANA-on premise system.

 

Click on Create(F8) and save the metadata file locally.

  1. **Create Service Consumption Model**:

   - In ADT, create a service consumption model.

   - Select RFC as the service type and upload the previously downloaded metadata file.

   - Follow the wizard to finalize creation.

  1. **Set Up Outbound Service and Communication Scenario**:

   - Create an outbound service with the type RFC.

 

- Create a communication scenario.

 

 

 

 

   - And in the outbound service tab, add the outbound service which is just created.

 

 

And Publish the communication scenario.

  1. **Administrator Configuration**:

   - Configure the communication system:

 

   - Set up the communication arrangement:

 

  1. **Call RFC Module in ADT Class Runner**:

   - Create a new ABAP class implementing IF_OO_ADT_CLASSRUN.

   - Copy the code from Service Consumption Model.

 

   - Save and run the class to test the RFC call.

 

 

Thanks,

Jaman

I am able to send email only when login with manager id . Manager user email is no-reply-it@npl.com. In General Settings Email Id is - npl.loc\no-reply-it . Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Analytics Average Calculation
entry.content:
Hi @Abraham3 ,

Good day!!

Averaging between region will give you only single value(which is the average amount considering all the regions). So when you again add the region dimension to the chart it will any way split to individual values.

You must remove the region dimension in the chart to get the average.

Or if you want the split of regions you should be aggregating based on other dimensions.

Kind regards,

Tha.Ram

entry.title: NOW RELEASED: Upload and work with external files in SAP Cloud ALM for implementation
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM streamlines application lifecycle management for SAP customers by combining efficiency and cost-effectiveness. With its automatic updates and easy setup, it eliminates the need for hardware investments, complex configurations, or technical upgrades. Best of all, SAP Cloud ALM is free of subscription fees. Under SAP’s fair usage policy, customers benefit from built-in resources, including 8 GB of SAP HANA memory and monthly outbound API data transfers, allowing businesses to focus on growth while minimizing technical overhead.

Previously, SAP Cloud ALM users could only reference external files stored in disparate locations using URLs. Given the widespread use of Word documents, PDFs, Excel sheets, and similar formats, managing documents efficiently was a challenge.

We are excited to announce that it is now possible to upload documents directly into SAP Cloud ALM for implementation through an integration with SAP BTP Document Management Service (DMS).

About SAP BTP Document Management Service (DMS)

SAP BTP Document Management Service is a cloud-based solution for secure and scalable document storage and management. It enables businesses to seamlessly store, manage, and share files across various SAP applications and processes. This integration aligns with SAP's strategy to provide harmonized and centralized document management capabilities.

More details about SAP BTP DMS can be found here:
Document Management Service Documentation 

How to Connect SAP Cloud ALM to SAP BTP DMS

To store external files in SAP Cloud ALM, customers must request:

  1. SAP Document Management Service, Integration Option
  2. SAP Document Management Service, Repository Option

Once these services are requested, a connection between SAP Cloud ALM and SAP BTP DMS can be established, enabling seamless document uploads and management. More details on connecting the service can be found in the Set up & Admin Guide of SAP Cloud ALM.

For SAP BTP DMS there are different Service plans available.

Documents managed via SAP Cloud ALM are stored in a dedicated repository. These documents can be uploaded, replaced, or deleted exclusively through SAP Cloud ALM, ensuring proper change tracking and preparing for future enhancements, such as versioning and document history.

Core Functionalities in the First Release

The initial version introduces essential capabilities:

1. Uploading External Files

Once the integration with SAP BTP Document Management Service (DMS) is established, users can upload one file per document in SAP Cloud ALM. To do this:

  • Create a document in SAP Cloud ALM.

  • Access the "External File" tab, where you can upload a file (maximum size: 10 MB) stored on your PC or another location. Supported MIME types can be checked in the SAP Cloud ALM application help.
  • Click “Upload” to store the file in the SAP BTP DMS repository. The repository operates seamlessly in the background, invisible to end-users.

  • The file name and size will appear in the External File tab. A draft version of the document is automatically created. If the draft is discarded, the file is also deleted. To finalize, click “Create” to save the document.

Uploaded documents are fully manageable within SAP Cloud ALM: users can assign a document type, owner, status, tags, and relate the document to processes or other elements.

In the list view, documents with external files appear alongside others, and new filters enable searching specifically for these documents.

2. Replacing External Files

To update a file:

  • Download the current version via the provided link.
  • Edit locally and use the "Replace" option while in edit mode to upload the new version.

If working in draft mode, the saved version remains accessible until the changes are finalized.

3. Searching and Copying Documents

File names are searchable from the list view, even when they differ from the document title.

Additionally, documents with external files can be copied, creating a new version in the repository that can be modified independently.

4. Deleting External Files

To delete an external file:

  • Switch to edit mode and select “Delete”. Only the file in the DMS repository is removed, while the document remains.
  • To delete both the document and its external file, save changes and delete the document in the standard way. Mass deletions can also be performed from the list view.

Looking Ahead

The first release marks the beginning of capabilities in SAP Cloud ALM working with external files. Planned improvements include:

  • Document Previews
  • Versioning and Document History
  • Transfer of Documents from SAP Solution Manager

Your feedback is very welcome — please share your ideas, questions, and influence requests to help shape the future of SAP Cloud ALM.

entry.title: E2E - OIL&GAS - Integration between TD and TM
entry.content:
In a conversation with some friends about the integration between TD and TM, some questions arose regarding the E2E process, so I will briefly describe what an Outbound process from TD to TM looks like.

Below is a flow with the steps. As we can see, all the steps are already known, however, they are organized a little differently than normal. 

Two moments are important to highlight:

1 Quantity Assignment

In this step, we can follow the manual path, executing transaction O4L1 - TD-F Doc Item Quantity-Create/Change, or in a massive way, through transaction OINSC_TDQTYA - Bulk Shpmt - Mass DocItem Qty Assign. This step is the main step to start the integration between TD and TM.

2 Create Freight Unit and Freight Order

These two steps are created automatically, without the need for user intervention. It is triggered after the completion of the “Quantity Assignment” step. In a standard TM scenario, Freight Order Creation is necessary for transportation planning; in this case, the planning has already been executed by O4F1- Create Shipment.

If you notice, we do not have the VI01 – Create Shipment Costs step, this step is no longer necessary in this new design, now this is the responsibility of TM, calculating the freight cost, creating the PO and SES.

I believe that with this additional information it is possible to have a complete view of the topic.

Here are the links to the other posts.

1 Functional Information: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-for-oil-gas-and-energy-blogs/oil-amp-gas-integra%C3%A7%C3%A3o-entre-td-e-tm/ba-p/13712286

2 Technical Information: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-for-oil-gas-and-energy-blogs/technical-oil-amp-gas-integration-between-td-and-tm/ba-p/13904981

 

Help SAP

1 Integration of IS-OIL with Transportation Management: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/0f4ab800d01c4366b0c9aaff06a64320/4ceebff75b5647fda977ec70032578a5.html

2 TM-Based Shipment Costing in IS-OIL - TD: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/0f4ab800d01c4366b0c9aaff06a64320/67f7d804e9fe4a688499c2528b120776.html

entry.content:

If I first create one combination in the Key Figure Selection, then edit and add a new one, the profile is successfully saved.

Where can this issue come from?

entry.title: Re: Vendor Payment Process After Item Is Sold
entry.content:
Hi 

What I understood that you buy stock from vendor and make GR to own. but you made payment to vendor when you sell it.

if it is a scenario. I would suggest to use vendor consignment Process. in this when you will make gr by 101 k movement it will show stock in your company premises but you will not be liable to pay it to vendor. when you need, you bring stock in your premises you will be liable to pay to vendor and then you sell it to customer. 

see this blog it may give you idea 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/vendor-consignment-process/ba-p/13257635

Let me know if you more information 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: Build and Run Multi-Tenant SaaS Solutions on the SAP BTP
entry.content:
Update December 2024

Business-ready artificial intelligence was the main topic at SAP in 2024. AI-driven capabilities and features paved their way into all SAP products based on the copilot Joule and the AI Foundation of the SAP Business Technology Platform. And of course partners can incorporate generative AI in their partner solutions as well.

In this spirit we added an example for a generative AI driven feature to our latest update on the Partner Reference Application. The example explains how to integrate and use the SAP AI Core service and the Generative AI Hub in our sample application "Poetry Slam Manager".

In addition to the AI-driven feature enhancements, we have adapted the ERP integration to avoid issues with extended data validations introduced with CDS 8, and added a number of minor fixes, enhancements and tutorial updates.

A happy New Year and all the best for 2025!

entry.title: Looking for SAP Order Management solution for sourcing and availability learning material?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Customizing technical baseline of SAP OMF and SAP OMSA learning journey.

Overview
Welcome to the Customizing Technical Baseline of SAP Order Management Foundation and SAP Order Management Sourcing and Availability course. This comprehensive program is designed to equip you with the essential knowledge and skills for architecting and integrating SAP environments, specifically focusing on SAP Northstar Architecture, good practice principles, cloud connectivity, data ingestion, and backend setup. Throughout this course, you will learn how to effectively utilize SAP tools and methodologies to create robust and scalable solutions.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, you will be able to:

  1. Understand composable ERP and the integration of subaccounts, roles, and ID providers.
  2. Connect cloud tenants to backend systems and manage data ingestion.
  3. Implement technical integration between SAP S/4HANA ERP and data ingestion.
  4. Set up and configure iFlows, replication models, and change pointers.
  5. Monitor data processes using Cloud ALM and configure AIF settings.

Prerequisites

  • Exploring SAP Order Management Foundation and Sourcing & Availability

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

is there any way to update version in ALM our current SAP Cloud ALM  for client is using 2308 SAP S/4hana public cloud solution?

how can we use latest version 2408 or upcomming 2502?

entry.title: Re: How to call RFC FM using Service Consumption Model in ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Thanks for the detailed aggregated procedure with screenshots.

All these communication objects with different names are confusing, especially when the communication scenario you have created is ZCS_RFC_BAPI, but your code shows Z_DEMO, could you fix it, if possible, please?

dest = cl_rfc_destination_provider=>create_by_comm_arrangement(
         comm_scenario = 'Z_DEMO' ).

For information, the SAP documentation gives more details about the code, options and "Specific Properties": Service Consumption via Communication Arrangements | SAP Help Portal (path to the documentation: Home > SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) > SAP Business Technology Platform > Development > Development in the ABAP Environment > Integration and Connectivity > Developing External Service Consumption (Outbound Communication)).

Note that SAP published a tutorial Generate RFC Proxy Classes | SAP Tutorials which is using "create_by_cloud_destination" which is tagged as deprecated in the SAP help, they "recommend using the communication arrangement approach instead" as you did.

cl_rfc_destination_provider=>create_by_cloud_destination( i_name = 'A4H' ).

entry.title: How can I prepare for the Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build certification
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build learning journey.

Overview
As a low-code/no-code developer, you can create advanced applications and SAP extensions with SAP Build Apps. You also have the capability to develop automations and processes using SAP Build Process Automation, integrating them with other applications through APIs. Additionally, you can design business sites and integrate applications within SAP Build Work Zone to enhance user engagement and streamline business processes.
Successfully completing this exercise confirms your comprehensive understanding and skills necessary to pursue the SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build certification as the next step.

Learning objectives

  • Interplay of SAP Build Products (SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Work Zone)
  • Confirm your proficiency and readiness for pursuing the SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build certification.

Prerequisites
To prepare for this exercise, it is necessary to take the following learning journeys

  • Developing Apps with SAP Build Apps using Drag-and-Drop Simplicity,
  • Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation
  • Designing SAP Build Work Zone Learning Journeys. 

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Is SQL Anywhere viable and sustainable
entry.content:
I wonder where you found the End of Main Channel Support?
In https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/sqlany/3362971125.html it just says "Active".
In the past there have been other EOL dates for SAP SQL Anywhere version 17, which got updated later on.
See also: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sql-anywhere-end-of-life-overview/qaq-p/13821802 

It would be nice if someone from the SAP SQL AnyWhere team could respond to this

entry.title: Re: Format for India Withholding Reports
entry.content:
Hi Moni123,

Please note the following withholding tax reports are available in SAP S/4HANA public cloud :-

  • 26Q (TDS Domestic)
  • 27Q  (TDS Foreign)
  • 27EQ ( TCS)

The above mentioned reports can be generated and downloaded from Run Statutory Reports app only in .TXT format. 

You need to download an utility (External software) to convert into FUV format upon your requirement.

Additionally, please note that the challan process has to be completed before generating the withholding tax reports.

Hope this helps.

 

Thanks and regards,

Atanu

entry.title: How to create attachments using API_CV_ATTACHMENT?
entry.content: I need to create to create attachments, from CPI, to a Purchase Contract document using the API API_CV_ATTACHMENT. Does someone already did this?

Re: Vendor Payment Process After Item Is Sold
entry.content: Thank you for your quick response and valuable insight. I kindly have a few follow-up questions. 1. For the retail industry with multiple stores, how can consignment stock be effectively managed across various locations? 2. If a customer returns an item after the invoice has been submitted, how can it be differentiated within the consignment process? 3. Regarding sales quantities, should we rely on reports, or is there a way to integrate this directly into the consignment process? 4. Are there any best practices for managing consignment settlement and tracking in a retail environment?

entry.title: Do you know the functionalities and concepts of SAP Field Service Management?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Implementing SAP Field Service Management learning journey.

Overview
This Learning Journey provides an in-depth understanding of the key business scenarios supported by SAP Field Service Management, while illustrating its main functionalities and concepts.
SAP Field Service Management supports dispatchers with real-time, automated workforce scheduling and planning, as well as providing service technicians with powerful apps to fulfill field service needs.

Learning objectives
After completing this intermediate level learning journey, Implementation Consultants will understand the full functionality of the web- and mobile applications offered by the SAP Field Service Management solution. They will understand standard product features, perform configurations and customizations using the Field Service Management Administration Module and they will know use case scenarios such as administrator, dispatcher, and field technician.
Furthermore they will be able to provide first level support and they know where to get additional information, best practices and documentation.

Prerequisites

  • Managing SAP Field Service Management

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

Hello @ARUPGHOSH

Sales Processing Using Third-Party Without Shipping Notification (BDK)

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

Dear,

How can I monitoring an ASE database with SAP Cloud ALM, please ?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Use of Account type option into Chart of account SAP B1
entry.content:
E stands for Expenses, I for Incoming, and N for Other.
Financial and income statements evaluate the type of account in the country's accounting plan to use them or place them in the appropriate places.

entry.title: Re: How to get the cancellation date for an invoice in VBRK?
entry.content:
I couldn’t find how to delete my own posts, but here’s what I’ve discovered:

The table VBRK has a column called SFAKN, which contains the number of the original canceled document. Additionally, in our specific implementation of SAP, the column VBRK-VBTYP holds the value ‘N’ for cancellation documents. Therefore, I used the following SQL statement:

 

 

  SELECT SINGLE fkdat FROM vbrk
    WHERE sfakn = @p_vbeln
      AND vbtyp = 'N'
    INTO _date.

 

 

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Is SQL Anywhere viable and sustainable
entry.content:
I"ve seen 2028 in various places.

This is copied from my product page:

 

Details for Product Version SAP SQL ANYWHERE 17.0
Official Name:SAP SQL Anywhere 17.0
Product:SYBASE SQL ANYWHERE
Product Category & Line:Technology Platform > SAP SQL Anywhere
Type of Product Version:Standalone
Release Type:Standard
Current Status:Unrestricted available 
SAP HANA Readiness: 
 

 

Ready  16.07.2015 Further Information
Release Plan
Release to Customer (RTC):16.07.2015
General Availability:16.07.2015
End of Mainstream Maintenance:31.12.2028

entry.title: Re: API-To retrieve ICOs from PO system
entry.content: @Madhu29 it takes two steps to get the details - 1st step is to query the list of keys, and the 2nd step is to invoke the service to read the details for each unique key. I don't have the specifics any longer because I don't have access to a PI/PO system.

entry.title: Communication error when sending with HTTP, Idoc to HTTP destination
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to send idoc to HTTP destination using background job. Idoc gets created but sending fails.

Communication error when sending with HTTP

The error code and the error message are: 411 /Connect to XXXXXX:443 failed: NIECONN_REFUSED(-10).

Same works fine when done manually, but not in job. Connection test is ok in SM59.

Cannot find anythig helpfull with error code 411...

Any ideas what might be the problem when sending in background?

Regards,

Pekka

entry.title: Re: How to get the cancellation date for an invoice in VBRK?
entry.content: To get an answer you should use a better tag such as SD (Sales and Distribution) or even ABAP Development

entry.title: Re: A Practical Guide to Naming iFlows in SAP Cloud Integration (SCI)
entry.content:
Hello,

how do you deal with Iflows that have several endpoints or are subprocesses that are called via direct process? Your example only display quite simple scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Mass processing of Business Partners using MDC in FIORI(Part-1)
entry.content:
Hi @Tahir_Aksoy ,

I see, I would suggest couple of things here.

1. Instead of using a file, please use the Mass processing for products app to select few materials manually and perform the update. This will eliminate any issues which might be there in the file format or the data.

2. Use file download functionality, Export products(F3251) to download a file, make your own changes and then upload again.

If it still doesn't work, then try to check as described in note 3098273 - Error - Changing worklist content failed in Fiori App F1047A.

Regards

Aniban

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Manage requests that are imported in STMS_IMPORT in CALM
entry.content:
I have CALM implemented and connected to my 03 environments and ABAP Systems (DEV-QA-PRD). I now need to manage requests created in CALM just as it was done with CHARM in Solution Manager.
Please guide me on how I can implement governance in my environment.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Manage requests that are imported in STMS_IMPORT in CALM
entry.content:
Dear customer, 

All that you need to know abouth the integration of SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario and CTS-TMS is documented in this SAP Community blog:

First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)

Best regards,

Dolores Correa

entry.title: Re: B1i Error OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space (failed t...
entry.content:
check this sap note:

Nota 2354463

entry.title: Re: Webhooks in SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
Hi @Saurabh_Kabra ,

thanks for the feedback. Webhooks are released and can be used. I adjusted the image and linked the correct video. There you can also find the procedure to setup the webhook.

Regards,

Johannes

entry.title: Re: SAC Trick - Have you ever seen $^2160?
entry.content: Thank you very much for this. Saved me a lot of time to understand my issue.

entry.title: Re: Not able to purchase one attempt for SAP certification exam for India region From SAP Training S
entry.content: I'm also facing same issue, created a case , waiting for their response

entry.title: Double slash (//) issue in Adobe forms
entry.content:
I have a text field in adobe forms layout. Value is filled dynamically from abap. When I fill data contains string "//+Character" then it considers this like a carriage return. But when I fill data contains string "//+Numeric" it is all good.

For Example

DATA 1:

//S/1231231231231231d.1231.231113,1231,31231231

Display:

//S

/1231231231231231d.1231.231113,1231,31231231

DATA 2: 

//1/1231231231231231d.1231.231113,1231,31231231

Display:

//1/1231231231231231d.1231.231113,1231,31231231

Why doest it go to new line after //S in DATA 1 ?

entry.title: Re: Not able to purchase one/six attempt for SAP certific...
entry.content:
Received a response from SAP after creating a case.

To "create a case" at SAP support here (at the bottom of the page): https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter/certification-support

entry.title: Re: CAP LLM Plugin vs GenAI javascript SDK + document grounding
entry.content: Following up to this question: if it is not discontinued: when should I use the plugin and when should I use the SDK? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Monitoring ASE database with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi,

Please review the links below, which tell us about the products and services Cloud ALM supports.

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/supported-solutions

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/landscape-management/lms-supp-serv-system.html

Also, a suggestion is listed for SAP for ASE on RISE if that applies to you then you can vote for it.

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/314002/?section=sectionDetails

regards,

Prithviraj

entry.title: Re: Not able to purchase one attempt for SAP certificatio...
entry.content:
Received a response from SAP after creating a case.

To "create a case" at SAP support here (at the bottom of the page): https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter/certification-support

entry.title: Re: Communication error when sending with HTTP, Idoc to H...
entry.content:
So basically, connection from SAP GUI (manually) is OK, but from Application server (nackground job) is KO?

There should be a gateway issue or IP address not maintained correctly. Check for the IP address. Then update it in central instance, application server and firewall (Also white list)

Also use tools such as the ICM trace, via transaction SMICM.

entry.title: CAP LLM plugin vs. AI SDK
entry.content:
Hello there!

There are several SDKs for AI Core existing (for example here) and the CAP LLM plugin.

My question would be: when should I use which? Are there important differences in their application areas?

Thanks and best regards,

Leonie

entry.title: Re: How to manage Workflows for Sales Price in SAP S/4HANA ?
entry.content:
Thank you Marcela! This post is very helpful.

I have a question: If the business case requires approval for conditions with values over a specific amount, let's say 1000 EUR, how can we manage that scenario? In the step pre-conditions I only saw the condition type, sales org and distribution channel. Couldn't find any possibility to trigger workflow bases on the value of the condition.

Thank you in advance for the help!

Roberto

entry.title: Re: LO_BCS Creates folder in client's outlook for PDF print.
entry.content: Thanks for the response Tomas, I have added code in main question. I observed this issue occurs only in web mail outlook (https://outlook.office.com/) not in outlook app. is it something because of Microsoft settings ? In outlook app I can see the PDF without any folder.

entry.title: Re: Double slash (//) issue in Adobe forms
entry.content: You'll need to escape the / character to get it to display how you wish.
